channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026: Funding Opportunity for Intercultural Exchange and Global Citizenship Projects Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Organizations working to promote intercultural understanding, global citizenship, international collaboration, and meaningful cross-cultural experiences now have an exciting opportunity to secure funding through the Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026. Lisle International has officially announced the opening of its Global Seed Grant application process, providing support for innovative projects that align with its mission of […]

The post Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026: Funding Opportunity for Intercultural Exchange and Global Citizenship Projects Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to promote intercultural understanding, global citizenship, international collaboration, and meaningful cross-cultural experiences now have an exciting opportunity to secure funding through the Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026.

Lisle International has officially announced the opening of its Global Seed Grant application process, providing support for innovative projects that align with its mission of building world citizens through intercultural engagement and international understanding.

The Global Seed Grant program is designed to help organizations launch, strengthen, and expand initiatives that foster meaningful connections across cultures, communities, and countries. Through a structured two-phase application process, selected organizations can access funding and personalized mentorship to help transform promising ideas into impactful projects.

Organizations interested in applying should prepare early, as the Request to Apply (RTA) submission window may close before the official deadline if a high volume of applications is received.

About Lisle International

Lisle International is dedicated to supporting a global community of changemakers by promoting intercultural experiences, international collaboration, and global citizenship.

Through its grant programs and international network, the organization helps individuals and institutions develop projects that bridge cultures, encourage understanding, and contribute to positive global engagement.

Its flagship Global Seed Grant program provides financial support and mentoring to organizations working to create transformative intercultural initiatives around the world.

What Are the Global Seed Grants?

The Global Seed Grants are competitive funding opportunities designed to support projects that align with Lisle International’s mission and values.

These grants aim to:

  • Promote intercultural understanding
  • Foster international collaboration
  • Encourage global citizenship
  • Support innovative educational initiatives
  • Build connections across diverse communities
  • Strengthen cross-cultural learning experiences
  • Empower organizations working for positive social impact

The program is particularly interested in supporting projects that demonstrate creativity, sustainability, and meaningful engagement among participants from different cultural backgrounds.

How the Grant Process Works

The Global Seed Grant program uses a two-phase application process.

This approach allows Lisle International to identify promising projects before inviting selected applicants to submit comprehensive proposals.

Phase One: Request to Apply (RTA)

The first step involves submitting a Request to Apply (RTA) through Lisle International’s online grant portal.

During this stage, applicants provide information about:

  • Their organization
  • Project concept
  • Expected impact
  • Alignment with Lisle International’s mission
  • Project goals and objectives

The RTA serves as an initial screening process.

Lisle reviews all submissions and identifies projects that demonstrate strong potential and alignment with the organization’s values.

Only applicants whose RTAs are approved will move forward to the second phase.

Why the RTA Matters

The Request to Apply is not merely an expression of interest.

It serves as a critical evaluation stage where reviewers assess:

  • Project relevance
  • Feasibility
  • Innovation
  • Potential impact
  • Organizational capacity
  • Alignment with grant priorities

Organizations should therefore invest time in preparing a strong and thoughtful RTA submission.

Phase Two: Full Proposal Submission

Organizations that successfully pass the RTA stage will receive an invitation to submit a full proposal.

This stage allows applicants to provide more detailed information about:

  • Project design
  • Implementation strategy
  • Expected outcomes
  • Budget
  • Monitoring and evaluation plans
  • Sustainability measures

The full proposal provides reviewers with a comprehensive understanding of the project’s objectives and potential impact.

Dedicated Grant Mentor Support

One of the most valuable features of the Lisle International Global Seed Grant program is the mentorship component.

Applicants invited to submit a full proposal are assigned a Lisle Grant Mentor.

What Does a Grant Mentor Do?

The mentor helps applicants:

  • Navigate the application process
  • Refine project concepts
  • Strengthen proposal quality
  • Align projects with Lisle’s mission and values
  • Clarify application requirements
  • Address questions and concerns

The mentor serves as a dedicated point of contact throughout the proposal development process.

This personalized support helps organizations present stronger and more competitive grant applications.

Funding Focus

While Lisle International emphasizes intercultural engagement and global citizenship, applicants should carefully review the eligibility criteria to ensure that their proposed projects align with the organization’s mission.

Projects may involve:

  • International education
  • Cross-cultural exchange
  • Global citizenship development
  • Youth engagement
  • Community partnerships
  • International collaboration
  • Cultural understanding initiatives
  • Educational exchanges
  • Leadership development
  • Social impact projects with intercultural components

Organizations are encouraged to explore Lisle’s eligibility requirements before beginning their applications.

Important Application Dates

Request to Apply (RTA) Opens

📅 5 June 2026

Request to Apply (RTA) Deadline

📅 30 June 2026

However, applicants should note that:

The RTA portal may close earlier than the advertised deadline if a high volume of submissions is received.

For this reason, organizations are strongly encouraged to submit as early as possible.

Notification of RTA Results

📅 Before the end of July 2026

Selected applicants will receive invitations to proceed to the full proposal stage.

Full Proposal Deadline

📅 1 September 2026

Only organizations invited after the RTA review process may submit full proposals.

Grant Recipient Announcement

📅 30 November 2026

Successful Global Seed Grant recipients will be selected during the annual Lisle Meeting and officially announced by the end of November.

Project Implementation Period

Funded projects are expected to be implemented during the following calendar year.

Project Timeline

📅 1 January – 31 December (Project Year)

Organizations should plan their activities within this implementation period.

Application Deadline Timing

Both the Request to Apply and the Full Proposal (for invited applicants) must be submitted no later than:

🕛 11:59 PM U.S. Eastern Time

Applicants should carefully account for time zone differences when planning their submissions.

Why Organizations Should Apply

The Lisle International Global Seed Grant offers several significant advantages beyond funding.

Financial Support

Selected organizations receive seed funding to launch or strengthen impactful projects.

Professional Mentorship

Applicants gain access to experienced grant mentors who provide valuable guidance throughout the process.

Global Network Access

Organizations become connected to a broader community of changemakers and intercultural practitioners.

Increased Visibility

Successful projects benefit from association with a respected international organization dedicated to global citizenship.

Capacity Building

The structured application and mentoring process helps organizations improve project planning and grant-writing skills.

Tips for a Strong Application

Organizations interested in applying should:

1. Demonstrate Clear Alignment

Show how the project directly supports intercultural understanding and global citizenship.

2. Focus on Impact

Clearly explain the difference the project will make and who will benefit.

3. Be Specific

Provide concrete objectives, activities, and outcomes rather than broad ideas.

4. Highlight Sustainability

Explain how project benefits will continue after the grant period.

5. Submit Early

Because the RTA portal may close before the official deadline, early submission is highly recommended.

How to Apply

All applications must be submitted through Lisle International’s official grant portal known as Submit.

Applications sent through any other method will not be considered.

Organizations should carefully review the eligibility requirements and preview application materials before beginning the submission process.

Contact Information

For questions regarding the grant program:

📧 office@lisleinternational.org

📍 Lisle International
539 W. Commerce St #1782
Dallas, TX 75208
United States

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026 present an outstanding opportunity for organizations dedicated to fostering intercultural understanding, international collaboration, and global citizenship.

With a supportive mentorship model, a transparent application process, and a commitment to empowering changemakers worldwide, the program offers more than funding—it provides a pathway for organizations to expand their impact and contribute to a more connected and understanding world.

Organizations with innovative projects are encouraged to prepare early and submit their Request to Apply before the application window closes.

For more information on this grant visit here 

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here 

The post Lisle International Global Seed Grants 2026: Funding Opportunity for Intercultural Exchange and Global Citizenship Projects Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026: Grant Funding for Global Health, STEM Education, and Economic Mobility Programs
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Eli Lilly and Company Foundation (Lilly Foundation) has officially launched its 2026 Open Call for Applications, providing funding opportunities for eligible charitable organizations working to improve health outcomes, strengthen education pathways, and expand economic opportunities for underserved communities. Through its annual charitable grants program, the Lilly Foundation supports nonprofit organizations whose missions align with […]

The post Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026: Grant Funding for Global Health, STEM Education, and Economic Mobility Programs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Eli Lilly and Company Foundation (Lilly Foundation) has officially launched its 2026 Open Call for Applications, providing funding opportunities for eligible charitable organizations working to improve health outcomes, strengthen education pathways, and expand economic opportunities for underserved communities.

Through its annual charitable grants program, the Lilly Foundation supports nonprofit organizations whose missions align with its strategic focus areas of Global Health, K-12 STEM Education, and Economic Mobility. The Foundation seeks to fund impactful initiatives that address pressing social challenges while creating sustainable opportunities for individuals and communities to thrive.

Organizations with innovative programs that improve healthcare access, strengthen STEM learning pathways, or enhance workforce and economic opportunities are encouraged to submit a pre-application during the 2026 funding cycle.

About the Lilly Foundation

Established in 1968, the Eli Lilly and Company Foundation was created to extend the charitable reach and social impact of Eli Lilly and Company.

The Foundation operates as a tax-exempt private foundation and supports charitable initiatives that align with its philanthropic mission of improving lives and strengthening communities.

Through strategic grantmaking, the Foundation partners with organizations that demonstrate measurable impact, innovation, and long-term sustainability.

Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026

The Lilly Foundation’s 2026 Open Call provides grant funding to eligible charitable organizations involved in initiatives that align with one or more of the Foundation’s core focus areas.

The Foundation is particularly interested in supporting programs that deliver meaningful outcomes for underserved and low-income communities while promoting long-term social and economic progress.

Funding Focus Areas

1. Global Health

The Global Health focus area supports charitable organizations working to improve access to quality healthcare services in resource-limited settings.

The Foundation aims to address healthcare disparities by supporting organizations that:

  • Improve access to healthcare services
  • Bring healthcare closer to underserved populations
  • Strengthen healthcare delivery systems
  • Address barriers to treatment and care
  • Reduce the burden of non-communicable diseases (NCDs)
  • Improve health outcomes in vulnerable communities

Priority Areas

Particular priority may be given to charitable initiatives focused on:

  • Cardiometabolic health
  • Diabetes prevention and management
  • Obesity prevention
  • Cardiovascular health
  • Healthcare system strengthening
  • Community-based healthcare solutions

Geographic Scope

Programs may operate:

  • In the United States
  • In low-income countries
  • In lower-middle-income countries

Organizations implementing innovative healthcare solutions in underserved settings are especially encouraged to apply.

2. K-12 STEM Education

The Foundation is committed to expanding educational opportunities for students from low-income communities through investments in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education.

Program Objectives

Supported initiatives may focus on:

  • STEM learning opportunities
  • Educational equity
  • Academic enrichment
  • Career readiness
  • STEM mentorship programs
  • Teacher development
  • Science and technology engagement

Target Population

The Foundation prioritizes students from low-income communities, particularly during:

  • Middle school years
  • Critical educational transition periods

Geographic Focus

This funding area specifically supports programs serving:

📍 Marion County, Indiana, USA

3. Economic Mobility

The Economic Mobility focus area seeks to increase opportunities for long-term prosperity and community well-being.

The Foundation supports organizations delivering programs that strengthen economic opportunity and improve quality of life for underserved populations.

Key Areas of Support

Eligible programs may focus on:

Workforce Readiness

  • Skills training
  • Career preparation
  • Employment support services
  • Professional development

Postsecondary Education Pathways

  • College readiness
  • Vocational training
  • Technical education
  • Skills-based learning programs

Housing Stability

  • Housing support services
  • Community development initiatives
  • Programs promoting long-term stability

Geographic Focus

Funding under this category is directed toward organizations serving:

📍 Marion County, Indiana, USA

Additional Community Support Initiatives

Beyond the Open Call funding categories, the Lilly Foundation also contributes to community strengthening through:

Employee Giving Programs

Supporting charitable donations made by eligible Lilly employees.

Disaster Relief

Funding organizations involved in:

  • Emergency response
  • Humanitarian assistance
  • Recovery efforts

Community Development

Supporting initiatives that address urgent local needs and strengthen community resilience.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants generally include:

  • Registered nonprofit organizations
  • Charitable organizations
  • Community-based organizations
  • Educational institutions
  • Foundations
  • Public charities
  • Organizations recognized under relevant charitable regulations

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • A clear charitable mission
  • Alignment with Lilly Foundation priorities
  • Strong organizational capacity
  • Measurable program outcomes
  • Sustainable implementation strategies

What Types of Projects May Be Competitive?

Organizations may be particularly competitive if they demonstrate:

Strong Community Impact

Programs that create measurable improvements in health, education, or economic outcomes.

Scalability

Solutions with the potential to expand and benefit larger populations.

Innovation

Creative approaches to addressing persistent social challenges.

Sustainability

Programs capable of generating long-term results beyond the grant period.

Equity and Inclusion

Initiatives designed to serve underserved and vulnerable populations.

Benefits of Receiving Lilly Foundation Funding

Successful organizations may benefit from:

  • Financial support for program implementation
  • Increased visibility and credibility
  • Opportunities to expand impact
  • Strengthened community partnerships
  • Enhanced capacity for growth and innovation

As one of the world’s leading corporate foundations, Lilly Foundation support can help organizations scale proven solutions and improve outcomes for communities in need.

Application Process

The 2026 Open Call utilizes a pre-application process.

Organizations interested in seeking funding should review the program guidelines and frequently asked questions before submitting their application.

Applicants are encouraged to ensure their proposed activities align closely with one of the Foundation’s designated funding priorities.

Apply Here 

Important Dates

Pre-Application Opens

📅 1 June 2026

Pre-Application Deadline

📅 3 July 2026

Organizations are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid technical issues or last-minute complications.

Why Organizations Should Apply

The Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026 represents an important opportunity for nonprofit organizations working at the intersection of health, education, and economic opportunity.

With a strong emphasis on measurable impact, equity, and sustainable change, the Foundation seeks to partner with organizations that are creating lasting improvements in the lives of individuals and communities.

Whether your organization is expanding healthcare access in underserved regions, preparing young people for STEM careers, or helping families achieve economic stability, this funding opportunity may provide valuable support to advance your mission and amplify your impact.

For more information on this grant visit here 

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY on here 

The post Lilly Foundation Open Call 2026: Grant Funding for Global Health, STEM Education, and Economic Mobility Programs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows & Scholars Scholarships 2026–2027: Study in Washington DC with Up to 100% Tuition Funding for Future Global Leaders
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Are you passionate about public service, international development, diplomacy, human rights, climate action, national security, social justice, refugee issues, or global policy? Do you aspire to build a meaningful career that contributes to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges? The Johns Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies (SAIS) is offering an exceptional opportunity […]

The post Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows & Scholars Scholarships 2026–2027: Study in Washington DC with Up to 100% Tuition Funding for Future Global Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about public service, international development, diplomacy, human rights, climate action, national security, social justice, refugee issues, or global policy? Do you aspire to build a meaningful career that contributes to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges?

The Johns Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies (SAIS) is offering an exceptional opportunity for future and current public service leaders through its prestigious Public Service Fellows Scholarship Program and Public Service Scholars Scholarship Program. These highly competitive scholarships support outstanding students pursuing graduate studies in international relations and public policy at one of the world’s leading institutions for global affairs.

The programs provide substantial tuition support, leadership development opportunities, exclusive networking events, and direct engagement with public service practitioners and policymakers. Whether you are an early-career professional seeking a fully funded master’s degree or a mid-career professional looking to strengthen your policy expertise, these scholarships can help accelerate your impact and career growth.

About Johns Hopkins SAIS

The Johns Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies (SAIS) is part of Johns Hopkins University, America’s first research university, established in 1876.

Located in Washington, DC, SAIS is globally recognized for excellence in:

  • International Relations
  • Public Policy
  • Diplomacy
  • International Economics
  • Security Studies
  • Development Studies
  • Global Governance

Its strategic location in the United States capital provides students with direct access to policymakers, diplomats, international organizations, think tanks, and development institutions.

What Are the Public Service Fellows and Scholars Programs?

The Public Service Fellows and Scholars Programs were created to support talented individuals dedicated to serving society through careers in public policy, government, nonprofit leadership, international development, diplomacy, and social impact.

The programs aim to:

  • Promote leadership in public service.
  • Support talented students from diverse backgrounds.
  • Encourage innovative approaches to policy challenges.
  • Foster engagement with public service practitioners.
  • Build a community of future changemakers.

Johns Hopkins SAIS specifically seeks applicants who are:

  • Culturally aware.
  • Adaptable.
  • Strategic thinkers.
  • Effective problem-solvers.
  • Committed to public service.

The programs welcome individuals interested in fields such as:

  • Human Rights
  • Diplomacy
  • National Security
  • International Development
  • Climate Change
  • Refugee Issues
  • Gender Diversity
  • Social Justice
  • Public Policy

Public Service Fellows Scholarship Program

Overview

The Public Service Fellows Scholarship Program is designed for highly talented graduate students pursuing the Master of Arts in International Relations (MAIR) at the Johns Hopkins SAIS Washington, DC campus.

The program supports individuals who aspire to build careers dedicated to public service and global leadership.

Public Service Fellows Scholarship Benefits

Selected Fellows receive:

1. Full Tuition Scholarship

Recipients receive:

  • 100% tuition coverage during the first year.
  • Renewal for the second year upon meeting academic requirements.
  • Funding for up to four semesters.

2. Exclusive Professional Programming

Participants gain access to:

  • Leadership lectures.
  • Policy seminars.
  • Public service workshops.
  • Special networking events.

3. Access to Influential Leaders

Fellows engage directly with:

  • Policy experts.
  • Public sector leaders.
  • Alumni.
  • Government officials.
  • International practitioners.

4. Career Development Support

The Office of Global Careers provides:

  • Career coaching.
  • Public service career guidance.
  • Professional networking opportunities.
  • Internship and employment support.

5. Community and Volunteer Engagement

Participants join social and volunteer activities designed to build:

  • Leadership skills.
  • Teamwork.
  • Civic engagement.
  • Professional relationships.

Public Service Fellows Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of any country.
  • Gain admission to the Master of Arts in International Relations (MAIR) program in Washington, DC.
  • Demonstrate leadership potential.
  • Show academic excellence.
  • Demonstrate commitment to public service through professional experience or academic work.

Special consideration is given to applicants who demonstrate financial need.

Public Service Fellows Program Requirements

Recipients must:

  • Remain full-time MAIR students.
  • Maintain a cumulative GPA of 3.4 or higher.
  • Participate in at least one admissions recruitment activity each semester.
  • Complete semester and final program surveys.
  • Submit the Office of Global Careers Employment Outcomes Survey before graduation.

Public Service Fellows Application Process

Applicants must:

  1. Apply for admission to the MAIR program.
  2. Indicate interest in institutional financial aid.
  3. Indicate interest in a Public Service Award.
  4. Submit:
    • Financial Aid Application.
    • Scholarship Essay.
  5. U.S. citizens and permanent residents must complete the FAFSA using school code E00474.

Public Service Scholars Scholarship Program

Overview

The Public Service Scholars Scholarship Program is intended for experienced mid-career professionals pursuing the Master of International Public Policy (MIPP).

Applicants should have prior experience in public service and intend to continue serving communities and institutions after graduation.

The scholarship is available only to Fall applicants.

Public Service Scholars Benefits

Selected Scholars receive:

1. Significant Tuition Support

Recipients receive:

  • Up to 50% tuition scholarship.

2. Exclusive Leadership Programming

Participants gain access to:

  • Public policy seminars.
  • Leadership workshops.
  • Professional networking events.
  • Public service discussions.

3. Career Advancement Opportunities

Participants receive:

  • Guidance from the Office of Global Careers.
  • Access to influential networks.
  • Mentorship opportunities.
  • Career development support.

4. Community Building Activities

Scholars participate in:

  • Volunteer initiatives.
  • Networking events.
  • Cohort activities.

Public Service Scholars Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of any country.
  • Be admitted to the MIPP program at the Washington, DC campus.
  • Be enrolled full-time or part-time (minimum of two courses per semester).
  • Demonstrate leadership and professional achievement.
  • Show commitment to public service.

Special consideration is also given to applicants with demonstrated financial need.

Public Service Scholars Requirements

Recipients must:

  • Begin studies during the Fall semester.
  • Maintain a GPA of 3.4 or higher.
  • Participate in recruitment activities or mentor Public Service Fellows.
  • Complete required surveys.
  • Submit employment outcome reports before graduation.

Why You Should Apply

This scholarship program offers far more than tuition support.

Participants gain:

  • Access to one of the world’s leading international affairs schools.
  • Study opportunities in Washington, DC.
  • Connections with global leaders and policymakers.
  • Leadership development experiences.
  • Career acceleration opportunities.
  • Public service-focused mentorship.
  • Exposure to real-world policy challenges.

Graduates are positioned for impactful careers in:

  • Government
  • International Organizations
  • NGOs
  • Public Policy Institutions
  • Human Rights Organizations
  • Development Agencies
  • Think Tanks
  • Diplomatic Services

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

Johns Hopkins SAIS strongly values diversity and welcomes applicants from:

  • Different socioeconomic backgrounds.
  • First-generation students.
  • Historically underrepresented groups.
  • Diverse cultures and nationalities.
  • All genders and identities.

The institution believes diverse experiences strengthen public service leadership and policymaking.

Information Sessions

Prospective applicants are encouraged to attend Public Service Information Sessions hosted by admissions representatives and current students.

These sessions provide insights into:

  • Academic programs.
  • Student life.
  • Scholarship opportunities.
  • Career pathways.
  • Financial aid options.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows & Scholars Scholarships 2026–2027.

Conclusion

The Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows and Public Service Scholars Scholarships represent a remarkable opportunity for individuals committed to creating meaningful change through public service. With tuition support of up to 100%, access to world-class education in Washington, DC, exclusive leadership programming, career development support, and a global network of policymakers and practitioners, these programs are designed to empower the next generation of leaders tackling today’s most complex global challenges. If you are passionate about diplomacy, human rights, development, public policy, climate action, or social justice, this scholarship could be the catalyst that transforms your career and amplifies your impact on the world.

Discover more global Scholarship and Fellowship opportunities on OFY.

The post Johns Hopkins SAIS Public Service Fellows & Scholars Scholarships 2026–2027: Study in Washington DC with Up to 100% Tuition Funding for Future Global Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 6 Neuroscience Travel Grants Open in June 2026: Funding Opportunities for Students, Researchers, and Early-Career Neuroscientists Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
For many students, researchers, and early-career neuroscientists, attending an international scientific conference can be a transformative experience. Conferences provide opportunities to present research, receive feedback from experts, build professional networks, discover emerging scientific developments, and establish collaborations that may shape an entire career. However, the cost of conference attendance—including registration fees, travel expenses, accommodation, and […]

The post 6 Neuroscience Travel Grants Open in June 2026: Funding Opportunities for Students, Researchers, and Early-Career Neuroscientists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
For many students, researchers, and early-career neuroscientists, attending an international scientific conference can be a transformative experience. Conferences provide opportunities to present research, receive feedback from experts, build professional networks, discover emerging scientific developments, and establish collaborations that may shape an entire career.

However, the cost of conference attendance—including registration fees, travel expenses, accommodation, and meals—can often create barriers for talented researchers, particularly those from low- and middle-income countries.

Recognizing this challenge, the International Brain Research Organization (IBRO) is offering multiple travel funding opportunities in June 2026 to support participation in major neuroscience conferences and scientific meetings around the world.

These travel grants are designed to help early-career researchers engage with the global neuroscience community, showcase their research, develop professional networks, and gain international scientific exposure.

This year, six major neuroscience travel grant opportunities are either open or launching soon, providing support for researchers from Africa, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, and other regions worldwide.

Why Neuroscience Travel Grants Matter

Scientific conferences play a critical role in career development.

Travel grants help researchers:

  • Present their scientific findings to international audiences.
  • Build collaborations with researchers from other institutions and countries.
  • Learn about the latest developments in neuroscience.
  • Receive mentorship and career guidance.
  • Improve visibility within the scientific community.
  • Gain access to new research opportunities.
  • Strengthen academic and professional networks.
  • Explore postgraduate and postdoctoral opportunities.

For many early-career researchers, a single conference experience can become a turning point that influences future research directions, funding opportunities, and professional growth.

1. Neuroscience 2026: International Society Member (ISM) Slots

Organizer

Society for Neuroscience (SfN)

Conference Location

Washington, D.C., United States

Conference Dates

14–18 November 2026

Application Period

15 May – 10 June 2026

Eligible Applicants

Applicants from:

  • Africa
  • Asia-Pacific
  • Latin America

About the Grant

This opportunity supports Society for Neuroscience international members from eligible regions who wish to attend Neuroscience 2026, one of the world’s largest neuroscience gatherings.

Participants will have the opportunity to:

  • Present their research.
  • Engage with global neuroscience experts.
  • Participate in scientific discussions.
  • Expand professional networks.
  • Explore international collaborations.

Key Benefits

  • Access to a major international neuroscience conference.
  • Scientific networking opportunities.
  • Research presentation opportunities.
  • Exposure to global neuroscience developments.

2. KSBNS 2026 Travel Grants

Organizer

Korean Society for Brain and Neural Science (KSBNS)

Conference Location

Daejeon, South Korea

Conference Dates

6–8 September 2026

Application Period

15 March – 12 June 2026

Eligible Applicants

Researchers from the Asia-Pacific region.

Maximum Funding

Up to USD 300.

About the Grant

The KSBNS 2026 Travel Grant supports early-career neuroscientists seeking to participate in one of Asia’s important neuroscience meetings.

The program aims to strengthen regional scientific collaboration while helping researchers gain valuable international exposure.

Benefits

  • Financial support for conference participation.
  • Networking with leading neuroscientists.
  • Opportunities to present research findings.
  • Enhanced regional scientific collaboration.

3. Neuroscience 2026: IBRO/SfN Travel Grants

Organizer

Society for Neuroscience (SfN)

Conference Location

Washington, D.C., United States

Conference Dates

14–18 November 2026

Application Period

15 May – 15 June 2026

Eligible Applicants

International applicants worldwide.

About the Grant

The IBRO/SfN Travel Grant provides support to early-career neuroscience researchers interested in participating in the prestigious Neuroscience 2026 conference.

The grant aims to encourage scientific exchange and facilitate engagement with the international neuroscience community.

Benefits

  • Participation in a world-class neuroscience conference.
  • International networking opportunities.
  • Exposure to cutting-edge research.
  • Professional development and collaboration opportunities.

4. Neuroscience 2026: IBRO/FUN Travel Awards

Meeting Organizer

Society for Neuroscience (SfN)

Conference Location

Washington, D.C., United States

Conference Dates

14–18 November 2026

Application Period

1 April – 21 June 2026

Eligible Applicants

International applicants worldwide.

Available Funding

Up to USD 1,000.

About the Award

This travel award enables researchers to attend one of the largest neuroscience meetings globally while gaining significant scientific visibility and networking opportunities.

Required Application Materials

Applicants must submit:

  • A Society for Neuroscience abstract.
  • Candidate statement.
  • Faculty sponsor materials.
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV).

Benefits

  • Up to USD 1,000 in support.
  • International scientific exposure.
  • Enhanced research visibility.
  • Access to global neuroscience experts.
  • Networking and collaboration opportunities.

5. 2026 Neuroscience Ireland Early Career Researchers Conference

Organizer

Neuroscience Ireland

Conference Location

Dublin, Ireland

Conference Dates

12–13 August 2026

Application Period

1 April – 30 June 2026

Eligible Applicants

International applicants.

About the Grant

This opportunity supports early-career researchers interested in participating in the Neuroscience Ireland Early Career Researchers Conference.

The conference provides an excellent platform for young scientists to exchange ideas, receive feedback on their work, and establish valuable professional relationships.

Benefits

  • Scientific exchange opportunities.
  • Networking with peers and experts.
  • Professional development.
  • Exposure to current neuroscience research.

6. Neuroscience Education and Neurotechnology in Nigeria (IBRO/NSN Travel Awards)

Organizer

Neuroscience Society of Nigeria (NSN)

Conference Location

Abuja, Nigeria

Conference Dates

7–10 September 2026

Application Period

1 June – 30 June 2026

Eligible Applicants

Applicants based in Africa.

About the Award

The IBRO/NSN Travel Awards support participation in the Neuroscience Society of Nigeria Annual Conference.

The initiative aims to strengthen neuroscience capacity building across Africa while encouraging collaboration and scientific exchange.

Funding Includes

  • Conference registration.
  • Accommodation.
  • Transportation.
  • Meals.

Benefits

  • Reduced financial barriers to participation.
  • Access to scientific training and discussions.
  • Networking with African and international neuroscientists.
  • Capacity-building opportunities.

IBRO 2027 Travel Fund: Investing in the Future of Neuroscience

In addition to the current travel grants, IBRO has launched the IBRO 2027 Travel Fund.

This initiative will support travel grants for early-career neuroscientists attending the:

12th IBRO World Congress of Neuroscience

Location: Cape Town, South Africa

Dates: 13–16 September 2027

The fund seeks to ensure that talented researchers are not excluded from scientific opportunities because of geographical or financial limitations.

By supporting the travel fund, donors contribute to creating a more inclusive and globally connected neuroscience community.

Why You Should Apply

Attending an international neuroscience conference can significantly accelerate academic and professional growth.

These travel grants provide opportunities to:

  • Present your work to global audiences.
  • Gain recognition within the neuroscience community.
  • Build long-term collaborations.
  • Learn from leading researchers.
  • Access mentorship opportunities.
  • Increase career prospects.
  • Develop a strong international scientific network.

For students and early-career neuroscientists seeking to advance their careers, these travel funding opportunities represent an excellent chance to gain valuable international experience and contribute to the future of neuroscience.

Application Deadlines

  • Neuroscience 2026 ISM Slots: 10 June 2026
  • KSBNS 2026 Travel Grants: 12 June 2026
  • IBRO/SfN Travel Grants: 15 June 2026
  • IBRO/FUN Travel Awards: 21 June 2026
  • Neuroscience Ireland Conference Grant: 30 June 2026
  • IBRO/NSN Travel Awards: 30 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the respective deadlines.

Official Website

For full eligibility requirements and application details, visit:

 

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post 6 Neuroscience Travel Grants Open in June 2026: Funding Opportunities for Students, Researchers, and Early-Career Neuroscientists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Position in Human Geography at the University of Zurich, Switzerland (2026): Join a Groundbreaking SNSF Research Project on Educational Inequality, Racism, and Classism
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Are you passionate about social justice, educational equality, youth studies, and qualitative research? The Department of Geography at the University of Zurich (UZH) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD Position in Human Geography as part of an innovative Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) research project examining institutional racism and classism within highly selective […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Position in Human Geography at the University of Zurich, Switzerland (2026): Join a Groundbreaking SNSF Research Project on Educational Inequality, Racism, and Classism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about social justice, educational equality, youth studies, and qualitative research? The Department of Geography at the University of Zurich (UZH) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD Position in Human Geography as part of an innovative Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) research project examining institutional racism and classism within highly selective secondary schools in Switzerland.

This exceptional doctoral opportunity offers aspiring researchers the chance to contribute to cutting-edge research, pursue a doctoral degree within a structured graduate program, engage with young people and educational institutions, and develop an academic career in one of Europe’s leading universities.

The position is offered at 80% employment and is scheduled to begin on 1 October 2026 or by mutual agreement.

About the University of Zurich

The University of Zurich (UZH) is the largest university in Switzerland, with approximately 30,000 students and over 10,000 employees. Renowned for its excellence in research, innovation, and teaching, UZH provides an intellectually stimulating environment where doctoral researchers can thrive.

Located in Zurich, one of the world’s most livable cities, UZH offers outstanding academic resources, international collaboration opportunities, and a vibrant multicultural atmosphere.

About the Research Project

The successful candidate will join the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF)-funded project titled:

“Institutional Conditions for Racially and/or Socio-Economically Minoritized Students at Highly Selective Secondary Schools in Switzerland.”

The project is led by Dr. Sara Landolt, Principal Investigator and researcher at the Department of Geography.

Research Context

Educational systems across many Western societies continue to reproduce social inequalities. In Switzerland, racially and socio-economically minoritized students remain significantly underrepresented in academically selective state-funded secondary schools known as Gymnasia.

Existing international research suggests that students from marginalized backgrounds often experience institutional discrimination, including:

  • Racism
  • Classism
  • Social exclusion
  • Structural inequalities
  • Barriers to educational advancement

Despite growing awareness of these issues, there remains limited research exploring how institutional racism and classism operate within highly selective educational environments.

Main Aim of the Project

The project seeks to deepen understanding of:

  • Institutional racism in education
  • Institutional classism
  • The intersection of race and socio-economic inequalities
  • Strategies for preventing discrimination
  • Practices that promote inclusive educational environments

The research adopts a post-structuralist processual perspective inspired by the work of Ahmed (2012).

Research Objectives

The study pursues several important objectives:

1. Investigating the Reproduction of Inequality

The project seeks to uncover the underlying mechanisms that reproduce:

  • Racism
  • Classism
  • Intersecting forms of discrimination
  • Resistance to discriminatory practices

within Gymnasia in German-speaking Switzerland.

2. Identifying Effective Practices

Researchers will explore successful initiatives already implemented within schools to address and reduce:

  • Racism
  • Socio-economic exclusion
  • Educational inequalities

3. Understanding Student Experiences

The project aims to understand:

  • How institutional discrimination affects students
  • How students respond to and navigate discriminatory environments
  • Strategies students use to cope with and challenge inequalities

4. Supporting Educational Change

Findings will be shared with school leaders to help create educational environments that are free from racism and classism.

Responsibilities of the PhD Researcher

The selected doctoral researcher will play a central role in the project and will work closely with Dr. Sara Landolt.

Key responsibilities include:

Research Activities

  • Designing and implementing project activities.
  • Conducting original fieldwork.
  • Carrying out qualitative interviews.
  • Working with school leaders and students.
  • Collecting and managing research data.

Fieldwork

The researcher will conduct qualitative interviews with:

  • Headteachers of Gymnasia.
  • Racially minoritized students.
  • Socio-economically minoritized students.

The fieldwork will primarily take place in German-speaking Switzerland.

Data Analysis

Responsibilities include:

  • Qualitative data coding and analysis.
  • Participatory data analysis workshops.
  • Interpretation of research findings.
  • Development of evidence-based recommendations.

Doctoral Dissertation

The successful candidate will complete a paper-based doctoral dissertation based on the project findings.

Academic Contributions

The researcher will:

  • Participate in project workshops.
  • Attend international conferences.
  • Contribute to scholarly publications.
  • Produce practice-oriented outputs.
  • Engage with stakeholders and educational institutions.

Teaching Responsibilities

The position also includes contributions to:

  • Human Geography teaching.
  • Geography Teacher Training Unit (GTT) activities.
  • Departmental service responsibilities.

Graduate School Participation

The doctoral candidate will participate in the:

Zurich Graduate School in Geography & Earth System Science

which provides structured doctoral training and professional development opportunities.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for individuals who are passionate about:

  • Educational equality
  • Social justice
  • Human geography
  • Youth studies
  • Qualitative research
  • Racism and classism studies
  • Institutional change
  • Participatory methodologies

Applicants should have a strong commitment to critical and socially relevant research.

Required Qualifications

Applicants must possess:

  • A completed Master’s degree or be close to completion.
  • Academic training in a relevant discipline.
  • Experience in qualitative research methods.
  • Excellent communication skills.
  • Strong teamwork abilities.
  • Interest in field-based research.
  • Motivation to engage with issues of inequality and justice.

Relevant Academic Backgrounds Include:

  • Geography
  • Sociology
  • Educational Studies
  • Youth Studies
  • Social Anthropology
  • Social Work
  • Related disciplines

Additional Advantage

Experience with participatory research methods will be considered a strong asset.

Language Requirements

Applicants must be fluent in:

  • German
  • English

Both written and spoken proficiency are required.

Benefits of the Position

The University of Zurich offers an outstanding package of academic and professional benefits.

Academic Benefits

  • Fully funded doctoral position.
  • SNSF-funded research environment.
  • Structured doctoral training.
  • Regular academic mentoring.
  • Career development support.
  • International conference participation.
  • Publication opportunities.

Employment Benefits

  • Salary according to Swiss National Science Foundation scales.
  • Dedicated office workspace.
  • Technical equipment and research support.
  • Access to university resources.

Work-Life Balance

  • Flexible working arrangements.
  • Part-time employment model.
  • Family-friendly policies.
  • Childcare support options.

Professional Development

  • Continuing education courses.
  • Language training opportunities.
  • Skills development programs.

Health and Wellbeing

  • Access to sports facilities through ASVZ.
  • Seasonal flu vaccinations.
  • Wellbeing and relaxation facilities.

Discounts and Additional Perks

  • Car-sharing programs.
  • Vehicle rental discounts.
  • Technology discounts.
  • Mobile phone subscription offers.
  • Hotel reservation discounts.

International Support

The university provides dedicated support for international staff relocating to Switzerland.

Living in Zurich

Zurich consistently ranks among the best cities in the world for quality of life.

Residents benefit from:

  • Safe and clean urban environments.
  • Excellent public transportation.
  • Rich cultural life.
  • International communities.
  • Access to lakes and mountains.
  • Outdoor recreation opportunities.
  • World-class educational institutions.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit all materials as a single PDF file in English.

Required documents include:

  1. Motivation Letter.
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  3. Contact details of two referees.
  4. Academic transcripts.
  5. Writing sample (such as an MSc thesis).
  6. Essay (400–500 words).

Essay Topic

Applicants should explain:

  • Their interest in researching racism and/or classism in education and their conceptual approach; OR
  • Their interest in conducting research with young people and the methodological challenges involved.

Application Deadline

Initial Application Deadline: 8 July 2026

Shortlisted candidates are expected to be notified by mid-July 2026.

Applications will continue to be reviewed until the position is filled.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their application through the University of Zurich recruitment portal.

For inquiries regarding the position, applicants may contact:

Dr. Sara Landolt

Department of Geography

University of Zurich

Zurich, Switzerland

This fully funded PhD opportunity offers an exceptional pathway for aspiring scholars interested in educational justice, qualitative research, and social transformation. Researchers seeking to contribute to meaningful societal change while pursuing doctoral studies at one of Europe’s top universities should strongly consider applying.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global PhD opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Fully Funded PhD Position in Human Geography at the University of Zurich, Switzerland (2026): Join a Groundbreaking SNSF Research Project on Educational Inequality, Racism, and Classism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD in Financial Technology (FinTech) Scholarships 2026 at the University of Edinburgh in Partnership with Lloyds Banking Group
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Applications are now open for two prestigious fully funded PhD studentships in Financial Technology (FinTech) at the University of Edinburgh Business School. Co-designed and supported by Lloyds Banking Group, these doctoral opportunities provide aspiring researchers with the chance to undertake cutting-edge research that combines academic excellence with real-world industry impact. The studentships are designed for […]

The post Fully Funded PhD in Financial Technology (FinTech) Scholarships 2026 at the University of Edinburgh in Partnership with Lloyds Banking Group appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for two prestigious fully funded PhD studentships in Financial Technology (FinTech) at the University of Edinburgh Business School. Co-designed and supported by Lloyds Banking Group, these doctoral opportunities provide aspiring researchers with the chance to undertake cutting-edge research that combines academic excellence with real-world industry impact.

The studentships are designed for talented individuals who want to contribute to the future of financial technology, artificial intelligence, machine learning, customer analytics, insurance innovation, and financial services transformation.

Successful applicants will join one of the United Kingdom’s leading research institutions while working on industry-relevant projects that address some of the most important challenges facing the financial sector today.

Application Deadline: 18 June 2026 (23:59)

Programme Start Date: September 2026

About the PhD in Financial Technology (FinTech)

The PhD in Financial Technology at the University of Edinburgh offers students the opportunity to conduct advanced research in areas shaping the future of finance and digital innovation.

Through collaboration with Lloyds Banking Group, one of the UK’s largest financial institutions, students will gain exposure to both academic research and practical industry applications.

The programme is particularly suitable for candidates interested in:

  • Financial Technology (FinTech)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)
  • Machine Learning
  • Data Science
  • Financial Analytics
  • Insurance Technology (InsurTech)
  • Risk Management
  • Customer Behaviour Analytics
  • Digital Transformation
  • Cloud Computing
  • Multi-Agent Systems
  • Decision Science

Available Fully Funded PhD Studentships

The University of Edinburgh is currently offering two fully funded PhD studentships.

1. Sequential Foundation Model for Customer Behaviour with Uncertainty-Aware Pricing Decisions

This innovative four-year PhD project will focus on developing and investigating advanced foundation models trained on customer event sequences.

The research aims to create a shared representation framework capable of supporting multiple business functions within financial services.

Key research areas include:

  • Customer behaviour prediction
  • Risk assessment
  • Pricing decision support
  • Foundation models
  • Machine learning applications in finance
  • Predictive analytics
  • Decision intelligence

The project seeks to improve how financial institutions understand customer interactions while making more accurate and uncertainty-aware pricing decisions.

Funding Value

Successful candidates will receive:

  • £25,000 stipend for the 2026–2027 academic year
  • Annual increase of 5% over the remaining three years
  • Full funding throughout the four-year programme

2. Secure and Governed Multi-Agent AI for Home Insurance Claims and Pricing

This fully funded PhD project will investigate how agentic AI systems can transform insurance claims management and pricing strategies.

The research will focus on developing secure, governed, and cloud-native multi-agent frameworks that support intelligent decision-making processes within the insurance sector.

Key research areas include:

  • Agentic AI
  • Multi-agent systems
  • Home insurance innovation
  • Claims automation
  • Insurance pricing
  • Cloud-native architectures
  • AI governance
  • Cybersecurity
  • Responsible AI

The project offers an opportunity to work at the intersection of artificial intelligence, financial services, and insurance technology.

Funding Value

Successful candidates will receive:

  • £25,000 stipend for the 2026–2027 academic year
  • Annual increase of 5% over the following three years
  • Full funding for the entire four-year programme

Why These PhD Studentships Stand Out

These studentships offer far more than traditional doctoral research opportunities.

Successful candidates will benefit from:

Full Financial Support

  • Fully funded doctoral study
  • Annual living stipend
  • Increasing financial support each year
  • Reduced financial burden during research

Industry Collaboration

Students will work on projects co-designed with Lloyds Banking Group, ensuring direct relevance to real-world financial sector challenges.

High-Impact Research

Researchers will contribute to emerging fields that are transforming global finance, including:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Machine Learning
  • Financial Services Innovation
  • Insurance Technology
  • Predictive Analytics
  • Decision Intelligence

Academic Excellence

Students will study at the University of Edinburgh, one of the world’s leading research-intensive universities with a strong reputation in business, technology, and data science.

Career Advancement

Graduates will be well-positioned for careers in:

  • Academia
  • Financial Institutions
  • FinTech Companies
  • Insurance Firms
  • Technology Companies
  • Research Organizations
  • Government and Regulatory Bodies
  • Consulting Firms

Eligibility Requirements

The University of Edinburgh seeks to award these studentships to the strongest candidates based on academic excellence and alignment with the proposed research projects.

Selection will primarily consider:

  • Academic merit
  • Research potential
  • Relevant educational background
  • Fit with the chosen research project
  • Motivation for doctoral study

According to the programme information, eligibility is open to all applicants, meaning both domestic and international candidates may apply.

Financial Benefits

Each successful PhD candidate will receive:

Funding Package

  • Full funding for four years
  • £25,000 annual stipend during the 2026–27 academic year
  • Annual 5% stipend increase over the next three years
  • Opportunity to conduct industry-linked doctoral research

This funding package provides substantial support for living expenses while candidates focus on their research activities.

Application Process

Prospective applicants should carefully review the project descriptions and determine which studentship aligns most closely with their academic interests and career goals.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Review the research project details thoroughly.
  2. Assess their academic background against the project requirements.
  3. Prepare a strong application demonstrating research potential.
  4. Highlight relevant experience in FinTech, AI, analytics, computer science, finance, insurance, or related disciplines.
  5. Submit all required materials before the deadline.

Given the competitive nature of these fully funded opportunities, applicants should focus on demonstrating both academic excellence and a strong alignment with the selected research project.

About Lloyds Banking Group

Lloyds Banking Group is one of the United Kingdom’s largest financial services organizations. Through its collaboration with the University of Edinburgh, the organization supports research that drives innovation across banking, insurance, customer analytics, artificial intelligence, and digital financial services.

This partnership ensures that doctoral research remains connected to practical industry challenges and emerging market needs.

Application Deadline

18 June 2026 (23:59)

Click here for more and to apply

Prospective candidates interested in conducting world-class doctoral research in Financial Technology, Artificial Intelligence, customer analytics, and insurance innovation should submit their applications before the deadline.

With full funding, industry collaboration, and access to world-class academic resources, these PhD studentships represent an outstanding opportunity for future researchers and innovators seeking to shape the future of financial technology.

Click here for more global scholarship opportunities

 

The post Fully Funded PhD in Financial Technology (FinTech) Scholarships 2026 at the University of Edinburgh in Partnership with Lloyds Banking Group appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Olympic Refuge Foundation Collaborative Action Grant 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Sport and Displacement Initiatives Supporting Refugees Through Climate Action and Gender Inclusion
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Organizations working at the intersection of sport, displacement, refugee inclusion, climate resilience, and gender equality now have an exciting opportunity to access funding and support through the Olympic Refuge Foundation (ORF) Collaborative Action Grant 2026. Launched through the Sports for Refugees Coalition (SfRC), this funding opportunity invites organizations to develop innovative, collaborative projects that use […]

The post Olympic Refuge Foundation Collaborative Action Grant 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Sport and Displacement Initiatives Supporting Refugees Through Climate Action and Gender Inclusion appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working at the intersection of sport, displacement, refugee inclusion, climate resilience, and gender equality now have an exciting opportunity to access funding and support through the Olympic Refuge Foundation (ORF) Collaborative Action Grant 2026.

Launched through the Sports for Refugees Coalition (SfRC), this funding opportunity invites organizations to develop innovative, collaborative projects that use sport as a tool for inclusion, empowerment, resilience, and social change. Successful projects can receive grants of up to USD 50,000 to pilot new initiatives that strengthen support for refugees and displaced communities while promoting meaningful refugee participation.

The 2026 funding call places a special focus on organizations operating in Latin America and specific regions of Africa, including the Horn of Africa, the Sahel, and the Great Lakes region. Through this initiative, the Olympic Refuge Foundation seeks to encourage cross-sector partnerships, foster innovation, and scale impactful solutions that improve the lives of refugees and host communities.

About the Olympic Refuge Foundation

The Olympic Refuge Foundation was established to support young people affected by displacement through sport. Working with partners worldwide, the Foundation creates opportunities for refugees and displaced communities to access safe spaces, develop life skills, improve well-being, and strengthen social inclusion through sports-based programming.

The Foundation believes that sport can be a powerful tool for healing, empowerment, protection, and community cohesion. By investing in organizations and initiatives that place refugees at the center of programme design and implementation, the ORF continues to build a global movement that leverages sport for positive social impact.

In 2025 alone, the Olympic Refuge Foundation reached more than 33,000 young people through its various programmes and partnerships.

What Is the Collaborative Action Grant?

The Collaborative Action Grant is a funding mechanism launched through the Sports for Refugees Coalition to support new partnerships and innovative projects that address challenges faced by refugees and displaced populations.

The grant aims to:

  • Encourage collaboration among Coalition members.
  • Pilot innovative solutions using sport.
  • Promote refugee leadership and participation.
  • Strengthen cross-sector partnerships.
  • Address urgent social and environmental challenges.
  • Generate scalable and sustainable models for inclusion.

The programme recognizes that some of the most effective solutions emerge when organizations work together, combining expertise, resources, and networks to create stronger impact.

Funding Available

Selected projects can receive:

  • Grants of up to USD 50,000.
  • Technical support through Coalition networks.
  • Visibility within the global sport-for-development community.
  • Opportunities to strengthen partnerships.
  • Potential pathways for future scaling and sustainability.

The funding is intended to support project design, implementation, community engagement, monitoring, and learning activities.

Priority Themes for 2026

The 2026 grant cycle focuses on two key thematic areas that align with some of the most pressing global challenges affecting displaced populations.

1. Promoting Gender Diversity and Inclusion Through Sport

This thematic area seeks projects that use sport to:

  • Advance gender equality.
  • Increase participation of women and girls.
  • Promote diversity and inclusion.
  • Challenge discrimination and harmful stereotypes.
  • Create safe and accessible sporting environments.
  • Empower marginalized populations.

Organizations are encouraged to design initiatives that leverage sport as a platform for social transformation and equal opportunity.

2. Mobilizing Sport for Climate-Affected Communities

Climate change is increasingly becoming a driver of displacement and vulnerability worldwide.

Projects under this theme may focus on:

  • Climate resilience.
  • Environmental awareness.
  • Community adaptation strategies.
  • Supporting displaced populations affected by climate-related crises.
  • Strengthening community cohesion through sport.
  • Youth engagement in climate action.

The Foundation is particularly interested in innovative ideas that combine sport, environmental sustainability, and refugee inclusion.

Geographic Priorities

While organizations from the Sports for Refugees Coalition may apply broadly, the 2026 call prioritizes concepts from:

Latin America

Projects supporting refugee and displaced communities across Latin American countries are strongly encouraged.

African Priority Regions

Special consideration will be given to projects operating in:

  • Horn of Africa
  • Sahel Region
  • Great Lakes Region

These regions continue to experience significant displacement challenges, making innovative community-based solutions particularly important.

Who Can Apply?

Applications must be submitted through collaborative partnerships.

Eligible applicants include:

Sports for Refugees Coalition Members

The lead applicant must be a member organization of the Sports for Refugees Coalition.

Partnership Requirement

Applications must be submitted jointly by:

  • One Sports for Refugees Coalition member organization; and
  • At least one additional Sports for Refugees Coalition member partner.

Organizations that are not yet members of the Coalition may join on an ongoing basis and become eligible to participate in future collaborative opportunities.

Refugee Participation Requirement

One of the most important components of the grant is meaningful refugee participation.

Successful projects should demonstrate:

  • Refugee involvement in project design.
  • Refugee leadership and decision-making.
  • Community consultation.
  • Inclusion of lived experiences.
  • Co-creation of solutions.
  • Accountability to refugee communities.

Projects that place refugees at the center of programme implementation are likely to be more competitive.

What Types of Projects Are Encouraged?

The Foundation welcomes innovative ideas that combine sport and social impact.

Examples may include:

  • Community sports programmes.
  • Leadership development initiatives.
  • Inclusion campaigns.
  • Gender empowerment through sport.
  • Environmental education through sports activities.
  • Refugee-led sporting events.
  • Climate resilience projects linked to sport.
  • Social cohesion programmes.
  • Youth engagement activities.
  • Sports-based advocacy campaigns.

Projects should demonstrate clear objectives, measurable impact, and potential for replication or scaling.

Learning from Previous Grant Recipients

The 2025 Collaborative Action Grant supported four projects, each receiving USD 50,000.

The funded initiatives were implemented across:

  • Lebanon
  • Germany
  • Cyprus
  • Greece

These projects focused on:

Bridging Communities

Initiatives that highlighted the role of cities and communities in promoting inclusion and social cohesion.

Sport Against Hate

Projects that used sport to combat:

  • Discrimination
  • Misinformation
  • Hate speech
  • Social exclusion

All funded projects demonstrated strong refugee participation and were developed through newly established partnerships between Coalition members.

The success of these projects illustrates the Foundation’s commitment to supporting collaborative, innovative, and scalable approaches.

Find a full list of SfRC members here, updated as of the launch of the call for
concepts: Sport-for-Refugees-Coalition-Members.pdf

Why This Opportunity Matters

Displacement remains one of the defining challenges of our time. Millions of refugees and displaced people face barriers to inclusion, education, employment, and community participation.

Sport has proven to be a powerful tool for:

  • Building confidence.
  • Creating safe spaces.
  • Promoting inclusion.
  • Encouraging dialogue.
  • Strengthening resilience.
  • Supporting mental well-being.
  • Bridging cultural divides.

By investing in collaborative initiatives that combine sport with climate action and gender inclusion, the Olympic Refuge Foundation is helping to build stronger, more resilient communities around the world.

Application Deadline

Organizations must submit their concept notes by:

5 July 2026

Early preparation is strongly recommended, particularly for organizations seeking to establish new partnerships and develop collaborative project proposals.

How to Apply

Interested organizations should:

  1. Review the official concept note and guidance documents.
  2. Ensure they meet Sports for Refugees Coalition membership requirements.
  3. Identify a partner organization within the Coalition.
  4. Develop a concept aligned with one of the priority themes.
  5. Demonstrate meaningful refugee participation.
  6. Submit the concept before the application deadline.

Organizations interested in future opportunities may also consider joining the Sports for Refugees Coalition.

Application portal: email concepts to applications@olympicrefugefoundation.org

Visit HERE to learn more about the Olympic Refuge Foundation Collaborative Action Grant 2026.

Conclusion

The Olympic Refuge Foundation Collaborative Action Grant 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for organizations working at the intersection of sport and displacement to access funding, strengthen partnerships, and develop innovative solutions that improve the lives of refugees and displaced communities. With grants of up to USD 50,000 available, the programme encourages collaborative action focused on gender inclusion and climate resilience while ensuring that refugee voices remain central to project design and implementation. For organizations committed to using sport as a force for social impact, inclusion, and empowerment, this funding opportunity provides both the resources and the platform to create meaningful, lasting change.

Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Olympic Refuge Foundation Collaborative Action Grant 2026: Up to USD 50,000 for Sport and Displacement Initiatives Supporting Refugees Through Climate Action and Gender Inclusion appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Coordination Officer – Gender Equality and Youth Development Job in India 2026: Join the United Nations and Drive Impact for Women and Young People
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Professionals passionate about gender equality, youth empowerment, international development, and United Nations coordination now have an exciting opportunity to contribute to transformative change at the national level. The United Nations Resident Coordinator’s Office (UNRCO), in partnership with UN Women and the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), is seeking applications for the position of UN Coordination […]

The post UN Coordination Officer – Gender Equality and Youth Development Job in India 2026: Join the United Nations and Drive Impact for Women and Young People appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals passionate about gender equality, youth empowerment, international development, and United Nations coordination now have an exciting opportunity to contribute to transformative change at the national level.

The United Nations Resident Coordinator’s Office (UNRCO), in partnership with UN Women and the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), is seeking applications for the position of UN Coordination Officer – Gender Equality and Youth Development based in New Delhi, India.

This full-time position offers experienced professionals the chance to work at the center of UN coordination efforts, supporting gender equality and youth development initiatives across the United Nations Country Team (UNCT) in India. The selected candidate will collaborate with multiple UN agencies, government institutions, civil society organizations, development partners, and international stakeholders to strengthen the implementation of sustainable development priorities.

Application Deadline: 27 June 2026

About the Position

The Coordination Officer – Gender Equality and Youth Development will work within the Office of the United Nations Resident Coordinator in New Delhi.

The role is jointly supported by:

  • United Nations Resident Coordinator’s Office (UNRCO)
  • UN Women
  • United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA)

Under the overall supervision of the UN Women India Country Representative and in close collaboration with the UNFPA India Country Representative, the successful candidate will report day-to-day to the Chief of Staff of the Resident Coordinator’s Office.

The position plays a strategic role in advancing gender equality and youth empowerment throughout the United Nations system in India.

Why This Role Matters

India hosts one of the largest United Nations presences globally, with 27 UN entities working across various development sectors.

The selected officer will support coordination among these agencies and help ensure that gender equality and youth development remain central to UN programmes, policies, partnerships, advocacy efforts, and reporting mechanisms.

The role contributes directly to the implementation of the United Nations Sustainable Development Cooperation Framework agreed between the Government of India and the United Nations.

Key Responsibilities

1. Support UN Coordination and Programme Implementation

The officer will support effective coordination of UN activities under the United Nations Sustainable Development Cooperation Framework.

Responsibilities include:

  • Maintaining communication and information flow among UN agencies.
  • Supporting implementation of UN work plans.
  • Assisting Results Groups and thematic coordination mechanisms.
  • Monitoring programme implementation and progress.
  • Supporting evaluation and reporting processes.
  • Preparing periodic reports and strategic updates.
  • Assisting with development of collaborative and joint programmes.
  • Supporting annual planning and reporting exercises.

2. Strengthen Gender Equality and Youth Development Programming

A major component of the role involves ensuring that gender and youth priorities are integrated across UN initiatives.

Responsibilities include:

  • Conducting gender and youth-focused analysis.
  • Reviewing research, reports, and statistical data.
  • Producing analytical papers and policy inputs.
  • Supporting strategic programming initiatives.
  • Preparing programme and project proposals.
  • Developing resource mobilization strategies.
  • Monitoring implementation of gender and youth initiatives.
  • Supporting field missions and documentation efforts.
  • Assisting other UN Results Groups with gender mainstreaming approaches.

3. Advocacy, Capacity Building and Knowledge Management

The officer will help strengthen awareness and institutional capacity regarding gender equality and youth empowerment.

Responsibilities include:

  • Organizing training programmes and workshops.
  • Facilitating knowledge sharing among stakeholders.
  • Promoting best practices and lessons learned.
  • Supporting advocacy campaigns.
  • Developing communication and learning materials.
  • Producing high-quality knowledge products.
  • Enhancing collaboration between UN agencies, governments and civil society organizations.

4. Stakeholder Engagement and Partnerships

The successful candidate will engage with a wide range of partners including:

  • Government ministries and agencies.
  • Civil society organizations.
  • Women’s rights organizations.
  • Youth-focused organizations.
  • Development partners.
  • International organizations.
  • Technical experts and advisors.
  • UN Headquarters teams.
  • Programme and operations staff across UN agencies.

Contract Details

Position Information

  • Position Title: UN Coordination Officer – Gender Equality and Youth Development
  • Location: New Delhi, India
  • Employment Type: Full-Time
  • Contract Type: National Personnel Service Agreement
  • Initial Duration: One Year
  • Renewal: Subject to availability of funds and performance

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A Master’s Degree in:
    • International Development
    • Gender Studies
    • Human Rights
    • Public Administration
    • Social Sciences
    • Or a related discipline

Candidates with strong academic backgrounds in development policy, social inclusion, and gender equality are highly encouraged to apply.

Professional Experience Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • At least seven years of relevant professional experience.

Relevant experience includes:

  • Gender equality programming.
  • Youth development initiatives.
  • International development projects.
  • Programme coordination and management.
  • Strategic partnerships.
  • Stakeholder engagement.
  • Policy development.
  • Advocacy initiatives.

Additional desirable experience includes:

  • Working with national or state governments.
  • Collaborating with civil society organizations.
  • Experience with international organizations.
  • Programme design and monitoring.
  • Knowledge product development.
  • Grassroots community engagement.

Required Skills and Competencies

The UN is seeking professionals with a strong combination of technical expertise and leadership capabilities.

Core Values

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

  • Integrity
  • Professionalism
  • Respect for Diversity

Core Competencies

Applicants should possess:

  • Gender sensitivity and awareness.
  • Accountability.
  • Effective communication skills.
  • Inclusive collaboration.
  • Stakeholder engagement abilities.
  • Creative problem-solving skills.
  • Leadership by example.

Technical Competencies

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Programme management expertise.
  • Administrative and operational coordination skills.
  • Strong analytical and reporting capabilities.
  • Financial and budgeting knowledge.
  • Information management skills.
  • Advanced computer literacy.
  • Excellent document preparation and presentation abilities.

Language Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • Strong spoken and written English skills.
