  • Proficiency in at least one national language.

Excellent drafting and reporting skills will be highly valued during the selection process.

Benefits of Working with the United Nations

This role offers an outstanding opportunity to:

  • Contribute to national and global development priorities.
  • Influence policies affecting women and young people.
  • Collaborate with leading international organizations.
  • Work within one of the world’s most respected multilateral institutions.
  • Expand professional networks across sectors.
  • Develop expertise in UN coordination and sustainable development.
  • Support implementation of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
  • Gain experience working across multiple UN agencies.

About UN Women

UN Women was established by the United Nations General Assembly in July 2010 as the United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women.

The organization leads global efforts to promote gender equality and women’s empowerment while supporting governments and partners in implementing transformative policies and programmes.

UN Women is committed to diversity, inclusion, equal opportunity, and maintaining a workplace built on mutual respect.

Application Process

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official UN Women careers portal.

Applicants are advised to:

  1. Complete all sections of the application form.
  2. Provide detailed education information.
  3. Include comprehensive employment history.
  4. Highlight relevant gender and youth development experience.
  5. Demonstrate coordination and partnership-building skills.

Incomplete applications may be disqualified during the recruitment process.

Application Deadline

27 June 2026

Professionals with a strong commitment to advancing gender equality and youth empowerment are encouraged to apply before the deadline and become part of the United Nations’ efforts to create sustainable and inclusive development outcomes in India.

Click here for more and to apply

Click here for more global job opportunities

The post UN Coordination Officer – Gender Equality and Youth Development Job in India 2026: Join the United Nations and Drive Impact for Women and Young People appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Position in Mobility Rights at Georg-August-Universität Göttingen (RTG 2987): Fully Funded Doctoral Research Opportunity in Global Migration Governance Introduction to the PhD Opportunity at RTG 2987
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Research Training Group (RTG 2987) at Georg-August-Universität Göttingen is offering a highly competitive PhD researcher position in the field of Mobility Rights in the Global Context of Multiple Crises. This doctoral opportunity is part of a structured interdisciplinary research program focused on understanding how migration agreements and global governance systems shape mobility rights in […]

The post PhD Position in Mobility Rights at Georg-August-Universität Göttingen (RTG 2987): Fully Funded Doctoral Research Opportunity in Global Migration Governance Introduction to the PhD Opportunity at RTG 2987 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Research Training Group (RTG 2987) at Georg-August-Universität Göttingen is offering a highly competitive PhD researcher position in the field of Mobility Rights in the Global Context of Multiple Crises.

This doctoral opportunity is part of a structured interdisciplinary research program focused on understanding how migration agreements and global governance systems shape mobility rights in times of political, economic, environmental, and humanitarian crises.

The position is designed for highly motivated researchers who wish to contribute to cutting-edge academic work in law, political science, migration studies, and international relations.

About RTG 2987: Mobility Rights in a Global Context

RTG 2987 is a structured doctoral research training group focusing on global mobility, migration governance, and legal frameworks shaping human movement across borders.

The program investigates how global crises impact:

  • Migration agreements between states
  • Legal frameworks for mobility rights
  • International cooperation on migration control
  • Human rights and border governance systems

PhD Research Focus Area

Applicants must align their research proposal with one of the core Research Areas (RA), specifically:

Research Area 11: Migration Agreements

This research area focuses on:

  • Bilateral and multilateral migration agreements
  • Global governance of mobility control
  • Legal and political frameworks regulating migration flows
  • Cross-border cooperation in migration management

Researchers are expected to develop strong, original academic proposals contributing to this thematic field.

Key Details of the PhD Position

The PhD position includes the following important conditions:

  • Position type: Doctoral Researcher (all genders eligible)
  • Duration: Up to 4 years
  • Employment level: TV-L E13 salary scale (75% position)
  • Start date: As soon as possible
  • Institution: University of Göttingen, Germany

This is a fully structured academic position embedded in a research training environment.

Financial Support and Employment Conditions

The position is financially supported under the German public academic salary system:

  • Salary is based on TV-L E13
  • Employment at 75% full-time equivalent
  • Long-term research funding for up to 4 years
  • Stable academic working environment within a structured doctoral program

Application Deadline

  • Deadline for applications: 30 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to ensure full consideration.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Academic Requirements

  • Must be eligible for doctoral studies in Germany
  • Must hold (or be close to completing) a Master’s degree
  • Strong academic background in relevant disciplines such as:
    • Law
    • Political Science
    • International Relations
    • Migration Studies
    • Social Sciences

Research Requirements

  • Must submit a strong research proposal
  • Proposal must align with RTG 2987 research areas
  • Demonstrated ability to conduct independent academic research

Additional Requirements

  • Fulfilment of doctoral admission requirements at the Faculty of Law
  • Willingness to participate in structured research training activities

How to Apply for the PhD Position

Applicants are required to follow a structured application process:

1. Prepare Application Documents

  • CV (academic resume)
  • Research proposal aligned with RA 11 or related themes
  • Academic transcripts and certificates
  • Motivation letter

2. Submit Application

  • Applications must be submitted according to instructions in the official job posting PDF
  • Ensure compliance with all formal requirements

3. Evaluation Process

Applications are assessed based on:

  • Academic excellence
  • Quality of research proposal
  • Alignment with RTG research areas
  • Fit with interdisciplinary research group

Apply Here

Important Contact Information

For academic questions:

  • Prof. Dr. Dr. h.c. Peter-Tobias Stoll

For general application inquiries:

  • RTG Coordination Office: rtg2987@uni-goettingen.de

About the Research Environment

The RTG 2987 program provides:

  • Structured doctoral training
  • Interdisciplinary academic supervision
  • Access to leading migration research experts
  • Participation in seminars, workshops, and public academic events
  • Collaboration opportunities across law and social sciences

Conclusion

The PhD position at RTG 2987 represents a prestigious opportunity for researchers interested in global migration governance and mobility rights. With long-term funding, structured academic training, and access to leading scholars, it is an ideal pathway for candidates aiming to build a career in academia, policy research, or international organizations.

For more information visit the official website.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post PhD Position in Mobility Rights at Georg-August-Universität Göttingen (RTG 2987): Fully Funded Doctoral Research Opportunity in Global Migration Governance Introduction to the PhD Opportunity at RTG 2987 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Claude Corps Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid $85,000 AI Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in the United States
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications Open for Anthropic’s Claude Corps Fellowship Program Opportunity Summary Details Information Program Claude Corps Fellowship Organizer Anthropic Location United States Duration 12 Months Salary $85,000 per year Eligibility 18+ years old with less than 2 years of full-time work experience Application Deadline July 17, 2026 First Cohort Starts October 2026 Relocation Support Available Number […]

The post Claude Corps Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid $85,000 AI Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for Anthropic’s Claude Corps Fellowship Program

Opportunity Summary

Details Information
Program Claude Corps Fellowship
Organizer Anthropic
Location United States
Duration 12 Months
Salary $85,000 per year
Eligibility 18+ years old with less than 2 years of full-time work experience
Application Deadline July 17, 2026
First Cohort Starts October 2026
Relocation Support Available
Number of Fellows 1,000 (across cohorts)

Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming workplaces across the world, creating new opportunities while also reshaping traditional career paths. To help ensure that the benefits of AI are shared widely, Anthropic has launched Claude Corps, a groundbreaking national fellowship program that will train and place 1,000 early-career professionals in nonprofit organizations across the United States.

Backed by an initial investment of $150 million, the program aims to equip nonprofits with AI expertise while providing fellows with valuable professional experience, hands-on training, and career development opportunities.

What is Claude Corps?

Claude Corps is a year-long paid fellowship designed to help nonprofits leverage the power of AI through Anthropic’s Claude platform. The initiative brings together talented young professionals and mission-driven organizations to advance social impact while preparing the next generation of AI-literate workers.

The program is a collaboration between:

  • Anthropic – Funding the program, providing AI expertise, and leading overall strategy.
  • CodePath – Serving as the employer of record and delivering fellowship programming and mentorship.
  • Social Finance – Leading monitoring, evaluation, and long-term sustainability planning.

The fellowship seeks to create a scalable model that demonstrates how AI can strengthen communities, organizations, and career pathways during a period of significant technological change.

Benefits of the Claude Corps Fellowship

Selected fellows will receive:

  • Full-time salary of $85,000 per year
  • Comprehensive benefits package
  • Intensive AI training focused on nonprofit applications
  • Ongoing professional development and mentorship
  • Dedicated support from CodePath mentors
  • Technical office hours with Anthropic experts
  • Access to an extensive Claude AI token budget
  • Professional supervision within host organizations
  • Opportunity to build highly sought-after AI skills

Fellowship Structure

The fellowship lasts 12 months.

At the beginning of the program, fellows will participate in intensive training focused on applying Claude and AI tools within nonprofit settings. Once placed with host organizations, fellows will continue receiving approximately five hours of weekly training, while spending the remainder of their time supporting their host organization’s mission and operations.

Host Organizations

Over the next year, more than 400 nonprofit organizations are expected to host Claude Corps Fellows.

Examples of participating organizations include:

  • Braven
  • Code the Dream
  • Heartland Forward
  • Montgomery County Food Bank
  • Team Red, White & Blue
  • Reef Environmental Education Foundation
  • SoundOff
  • StriveTogether
  • YMCA of Greater Charlotte

These organizations work across sectors including education, workforce development, food security, veteran support, conservation, mental health, and community development.

Who Can Apply?

The Claude Corps Fellowship is open to individuals who:

  • Are at least 18 years old
  • Have less than two years of full-time work experience
  • Are authorized to work in the United States
  • Are comfortable working with AI tools such as Claude
  • Are willing to relocate if necessary (relocation support is available)

Importantly, applicants from all educational backgrounds are encouraged to apply. A college degree is not required.

Fellowship Timeline

First Cohort

  • Application Deadline: July 17, 2026
  • Fellowship Start Date: October 2026
  • Number of Fellows: 100

Future Cohorts

  • January 2027
  • August 2027

Applications for future cohorts are being accepted on a rolling basis.

Why Claude Corps Matters

As AI continues to transform industries, many experts have raised concerns about workforce disruption and unequal access to emerging technologies. Claude Corps represents a proactive effort to address these challenges by investing directly in people and organizations that can benefit from AI adoption.

The initiative aims to:

  • Expand AI literacy among early-career professionals
  • Strengthen nonprofit effectiveness and impact
  • Create new career pathways in the AI economy
  • Generate evidence on how AI can support mission-driven work
  • Develop a scalable model that can be replicated nationally and internationally

Anthropic plans to rigorously evaluate the program’s impact on both fellows and host organizations, with the goal of expanding the model beyond the initial 1,000 participants and potentially adapting it for use in other countries.

How to Apply

Applications for the Claude Corps Fellowship are now open. Interested candidates should prepare their applications before the July 17, 2026 deadline for the first cohort.

This is a unique opportunity for aspiring professionals interested in artificial intelligence, social impact, nonprofit innovation, and community development to gain hands-on experience while earning a competitive salary and helping organizations advance their missions.

Applications can be submitted here. The application asks for:

  • Background and experience
  • Short-answer questions, including one about a time the applicant made an impact in their community, and one about a time they learned from a mistake or setback
  • Completion of two short AI training modules (AI Fluency and Claude 101) hosted on Skilljar—this gives applicants a preview of the training fellows receive, and helps the program see how they prompt, evaluate an AI’s response, and adjust when the first answer is not quite right (instructions are included in the application)
  • Demographic information (voluntary, used for program reporting and equity tracking)

PROSPECTIVE FELLOWS SHOULD CLICK AND FIND MORE HERE 

PROSPECTIVE HOSTS CAN FIND MORE HERE 


Looking for more fully funded fellowships and IA related opportunities ? Click here https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=AI

The post Claude Corps Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid $85,000 AI Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
